# google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy

A Zone VM Extension Policy.



<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=compute_zone_vm_extension_policy_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Compute Zone Vm Extension Policy Basic


```hcl
resource "google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy" "ops_agent_policy" {
  name        = "zonal-ops-agent-vme-policy-%{random_suffix}"
  zone        = "us-central1-a"

  extension_policies {
    extension_name = "ops-agent"
    pinned_version = "2.66.0"
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the resource. Provided by the client when the resource is created.

* `extension_policies` -
  (Required)
  A map of extension names (for example, "ops-agent") to their corresponding policy configurations.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_extension_policies).

* `zone` -
  (Required)
  Name of the zone for this request.


* `description` -
  (Optional)
  An optional description of this resource.

* `instance_selectors` -
  (Optional)
  Selectors to target VMs for this policy.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_instance_selectors).

* `priority` -
  (Optional)
  Priority of this policy.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_extension_policies"></a>The `extension_policies` block supports:

* `extension_name` - (Required) The identifier for this object. Format specified above.

* `pinned_version` -
  (Optional)
  The specific version of the extension to install.

* `string_config` -
  (Optional)
  String-based configuration data for the extension.

<a name="nested_instance_selectors"></a>The `instance_selectors` block supports:

* `label_selector` -
  (Optional)
  LabelSelector matches VM labels.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_instance_selectors_label_selector).


<a name="nested_instance_selectors_label_selector"></a>The `label_selector` block supports:

* `inclusion_labels` -
  (Optional)
  A map of key-value pairs representing VM labels.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/zones/{{zone}}/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}`

* `creation_timestamp` -
  Creation timestamp in RFC3339 text format.

* `id` -
  The unique identifier for the resource.

* `kind` -
  Type of the resource.

* `managed_by_global` -
  Indicates if this policy is managed by a global policy.

* `state` -
  Current state of the policy.


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


ZoneVmExtensionPolicy can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/zones/{{zone}}/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}`
* `{{project}}/{{zone}}/{{name}}`
* `{{zone}}/{{name}}`
* `{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import ZoneVmExtensionPolicy using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    name = "<-required value->"
    zone = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import ZoneVmExtensionPolicy using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/zones/{{zone}}/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}"
  to = google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), ZoneVmExtensionPolicy can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy.default projects/{{project}}/zones/{{zone}}/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy.default {{project}}/{{zone}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy.default {{zone}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_zone_vm_extension_policy.default {{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
